task.title:
# AutorIA — Implementation Prompt: Issue #43 "Style DNA" screen (radar + 2D scatter + metrics)

> **For:** Bob (implementation agent)
> **Repo:** autorIA · **Branch:** create `feat/front-style-dna` from `test/ui-design`
> **Milestone:** Sprint 1 — Foundation + StyleProfile · **Labels:** frontend, size:L
> **Issue text:** "Create the Style DNA visualization page. Render a Recharts Radar chart for
> lexical/syntactic metrics and a 2D Scatter plot for the semantic UMAP cluster."

---

## 0. Mission

Turn the placeholder on `/author/[id]` ("Full studio coming soon.") into the **Style DNA
panel**: a collapsible panel showing (a) corpus **MetricChips**, (b) a **Recharts Radar**
of six normalized style metrics, and (c) a **2D scatter** of the semantic UMAP space with
the selected author's cluster highlighted. Everything must follow `docs/design-system.md`
(the binding design guide), `docs/api_contract.yaml` (the locked API contract), and the
project conventions already visible in `frontend/src/components/`.

**Do not create new routes.** The app has exactly three routes (`/`, `/author/[id]`,
`/verify`). The Style DNA panel mounts inside the existing `/author/[id]` page.

---

## 1. Required reading (in this order, before writing any code)

1. `docs/design-system.md` — §2.4 (author palette + `--voice` indirection + `--chart-*`
   mapping), §3 (type scale recipes), §5 (motion), §7 (component inventory + the 7 rules
   for adding a component), §8 (5-second contrast playbook), §9 (decision log).
2. `docs/api_contract.yaml` — `GET /api/authors/{author_id}/style-profile` and the
   `StyleProfile`, `CorpusStats`, `LexicalFeatures`, `SyntacticFeatures`,
   `StylisticFeatures`, `DistinctiveTerm`, `EmbeddingUmap2d` schemas.
3. Reference implementations (copy their patterns exactly):
   - `frontend/src/components/FitScoreBar.tsx` — discriminated props, single source of
     truth for thresholds, AA-safe class maps.
   - `frontend/src/components/AuthorColumn.tsx` — the canonical async-state pattern
     (loading skeleton + status text / error + retry / success) and `data-voice` usage.
   - `frontend/src/lib/api.ts` — typed fetch client (`parseOrThrow`, `API_BASE`) and the
     CONTRACT GAP comment style.
   - `frontend/src/lib/authors.ts` — the demo-safe API-with-seed-fallback pattern
     (see decision log entry "API-with-seed-fallback").
4. `frontend/src/lib/i18n/en.ts` — string conventions (`as const`, typed interpolation
   functions, grouped by screen).

---

## 2. Contract facts you must honor

`GET /api/authors/{author_id}/style-profile` returns `StyleProfile` v1.0:

| Field | Shape | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `schema_version` | `"1.0"` const | |
| `author_id` | string | |
| `computed_at` | ISO date-time string | |
| `corpus_stats` | `{ n_documents, n_tokens, n_sentences }` (ints ≥ 0) | → MetricChips |
| `lexical` | `{ mattr_500, avg_word_length, hapax_ratio }` (numbers) | → radar axes |
| `syntactic` | `{ avg_sentence_length_tokens, std_sentence_length_tokens, subordination_ratio, passive_voice_ratio, noun_to_verb_ratio }` | → radar axes |
| `stylistic` | `{ punct_distribution: Record<string, number>, pos_distribution: Record<string, number>, dialogue_ratio, first_person_ratio }` | → radar axis (dialogue) |
| `distinctive_vocab` | `DistinctiveTerm[]` (already typed in `lib/types.ts`) | NOT rendered in this issue |
| `semantic_centroid` | `number[]` (768 floats) | NOT rendered — never ship it to a chart |
| `embedding_umap_2d` | `{ centroid: [number, number], spread: number }` | → scatter |

`404` means **author unknown OR StyleProfile not yet computed** — that is an *empty*
state ("not computed yet"), never a destructive error.

⚠️ **Scatter data limitation (do not work around it by faking data):** the contract
exposes only **one centroid + one spread per author** — there are no per-chunk points.
The scatter therefore plots **one point per author with a computed profile** (fetch the
author list, then fetch each author's profile in parallel, silently skipping 404s), with
the selected author's point in its voice color and a translucent dispersion ring scaled
by `spread`; all other authors render muted. This matches design-system §7
("selected author's cluster in its voice color, others muted") and the honesty rule
(§8.6). If you believe per-chunk points are needed, flag it as a contract gap in the
decision log — do not invent points.

---

## 3. Deliverables (files)

All components: `frontend/src/components/PascalCase.tsx`, one component per file,
< 500 lines, no `as any`, no new dependencies (Recharts `^3.9.2` and lucide-react are
already installed — note the **Recharts v3** API).

1. **`src/lib/types.ts`** — add mirrors of the contract, exactly named:
   `CorpusStats`, `LexicalFeatures`, `SyntacticFeatures`, `StylisticFeatures`,
   `EmbeddingUmap2d`, `StyleProfile`. JSDoc each with the contract reference, matching
   the existing file's style. `DistinctiveTerm` already exists — reuse it.

2. **`src/lib/api.ts`** — add `getStyleProfile(authorId: string): Promise<StyleProfile>`
   (GET, `cache: "no-store"`, via `parseOrThrow`). Throw a distinguishable error for 404
   (e.g. a small `NotFoundError` class or a typed result) so the panel can separate
   *not-computed* (empty state) from *server/network failure* (error state + retry).

3. **`src/lib/style-dna.ts`** (new, pure logic — no JSX):
   - `RADAR_AXES`: the single source of truth for the six radar axes. Each entry:
     `{ key, labelKey, domain: [min, max], select: (p: StyleProfile) => number }`.
     Axes (lexical/syntactic/stylistic mix, per issue):
     | Axis | Source field | Normalization domain |
     |---|---|---|
     | Vocabulary richness | `lexical.mattr_500` | `[0, 1]` (native) |
     | Rare words | `lexical.hapax_ratio` | `[0, 1]` (native) |
     | Word length | `lexical.avg_word_length` | `[3, 8]` chars |
     | Sentence length | `syntactic.avg_sentence_length_tokens` | `[5, 40]` tokens |
     | Subordination | `syntactic.subordination_ratio` | `[0, 1]` (native) |
     | Dialogue | `stylistic.dialogue_ratio` | `[0, 1]` (native) |
   - `normalizeAxis(value, domain)`: linear map to `[0, 1]`, clamped. Document each
     non-native domain with a comment (these are honest presentation scalings, not data
     changes — like `getFitBand`, thresholds live in exactly one place).
   - `CHART_COLOR_BY_AUTHOR`: `austen → "var(--chart-1)"`, `dickens → "var(--chart-2)"`,
     `poe → "var(--chart-3)"`, fallback (unknown/uploaded author) → `"var(--chart-4)"`;
     muted/other series → `"var(--chart-5)"`. **Recharts colors come only from these five
     tokens** (design-system §2.4) — pass them as `stroke`/`fill` strings; CSS variables
     resolve fine in SVG attributes, and they adapt to dark mode for free.

4. **`src/components/MetricChip.tsx`** — tiny presentational component:
   `font-mono tabular-nums` value + `text-xs text-muted-foreground` sans label
   (design-system §3 "Metric" + "Caption" recipes). Used for `n_documents`, `n_tokens`
   (format with `toLocaleString("en-US")`), `n_sentences`.

5. **`src/components/EmptyState.tsx`** — generic, reusable (it's in the Sprint 1
   inventory for *all* screens): lucide icon + one-line message + optional action
   (`ReactNode`). No async logic.

6. **`src/components/StyleRadarChart.tsx`** (`"use client"`) — Recharts
   `RadarChart` inside `ResponsiveContainer` at a fixed height (e.g. `h-72`); six axes
   from `RADAR_AXES`, values pre-normalized to `[0, 1]`, `domain={[0, 1]}`, radius axis
   ticks hidden or minimal. Series stroke/fill = the selected author's chart token,
   fill at low opacity (~0.25). Grid/axis text uses `var(--border)` /
   `var(--muted-foreground)`. Axis labels from `en.ts`. Container gets `role="img"` +
   a descriptive `aria-label` from `en.ts`; the MetricChips + a visually rendered
   axis-value list (or the tooltip) keep the data accessible as text.

7. **`src/components/StyleScatter2D.tsx`** (`"use client"`) — Recharts `ScatterChart`:
   one `Scatter` series per author profile provided via props
   (`{ authorId, authorName, point: [x, y], spread, selected }[]`). Selected author:
   voice chart token + dispersion ring (SVG circle sized by `spread`, ~15% opacity
   fill); others: `var(--chart-5)` muted dots. Both X and Y axes hidden (UMAP axes are
   meaningless to users — show a caption from `en.ts` explaining the map instead).
   Label each point with the author name (small `text-xs` label or Recharts
   `LabelList`) so color is never the only signal (a11y floor, design-system §7 rule 7).

8. **`src/components/StyleDnaPanel.tsx`** (`"use client"`) — the wrapper that **owns all
   fetch states** (design-system §7 inventory):
   - Props: `{ authorId: string; authorName: string }` — no fetching in the server page
     beyond what already exists.
   - On mount (and on Retry), fetch the selected author's profile **and** the comparison
     set for the scatter (author list → parallel `getStyleProfile`, skip failures).
     The panel renders as soon as the selected author's profile resolves; comparison
     points may stream in after (single state update is fine — no waterfall spinners).
   - **States (all four, no infinite spinner):**
     - `loading` — pulsing muted skeleton bars + status text (copy `AuthorColumn`'s
       skeleton pattern), plus a timeout guard (~10 s → error state with timeout copy).
     - `empty` — 404 / `has_style_profile === false` → `EmptyState` with `Feather`
       icon and "not yet computed" copy (this is *neutral*, not destructive — a freshly
       uploaded author lands here while the pipeline runs).
     - `error` — network/5xx → message + Retry `Button` (reuse the `StateNotice`
       pattern from `AuthorColumn`; `TriangleAlert`, destructive tone).
     - `ready` — layout below.
   - **Collapsible:** panel header row = section label ("Style DNA", design-system §3
     "Section" recipe) + `ChevronDown` toggle (rotate 180° with
     `transition-transform duration-200`), `aria-expanded` + `aria-controls` on the
     button. Default **expanded**. Collapse hides content only (conditional render or
     `hidden` — never animate height, design-system §5).
   - **Ready layout (desktop-first 1280, inside the existing `max-w-5xl` shell):**
     `Card` with `shadow-paper`; header row of MetricChips + `computed_at` caption
     (`font-mono text-xs text-muted-foreground`, format date only); below, a
     two-column grid `md:grid-cols-2 gap-6`: radar left, scatter right, each with a
     §3 "Section" label and a one-line caption from `en.ts`. Content fades in with
     `.animate-fade-rise`.

9. **`src/app/author/[id]/page.tsx`** — replace the `comingSoon` placeholder with
   `<StyleDnaPanel authorId={author.id} authorName={author.name} />`. Keep the
   existing `data-voice={author.id}` wrapper, back link, and serif `text-4xl` h1
   untouched. Remove `authorDetail.comingSoon` from `en.ts` only if nothing else uses it.

10. **`src/lib/i18n/en.ts`** — add a `styleDna` group. Every visible string, including:
    section title ("Style DNA"), collapse toggle aria-label, metric labels (documents /
    tokens / sentences), the six radar axis labels, radar aria-label + caption, scatter
    caption (e.g. "Semantic map — each point is an author's corpus centroid; the ring
    shows dispersion"), loading status, empty title + body, error message, retry, and a
    `computedAt: (date: string) => ...` interpolation. Keep `as const` and the `EN`
    type export intact. **Zero hardcoded English in JSX.**

11. **`docs/design-system.md`** — same PR: mark `StyleDnaPanel`, `StyleRadarChart`,
    `StyleScatter2D`, `MetricChip`, `EmptyState` as ✅ built in the §7 inventory, and add
    decision-log rows for (a) the six radar axes + normalization domains, (b) the
    centroid-only scatter (contract limitation), (c) the fixture fallback below if you
    implement it.

---

## 4. Demo-safety (backend is not there yet)

The backend currently implements only `GET /api/authors` and `/health` — the
style-profile endpoint will 404/refuse for now. Follow the established
API-with-seed-fallback decision: add `src/lib/fixtures/style-profiles.ts` with **typed
`StyleProfile` fixtures for the three seed authors** (plausible, clearly-commented
values; centroids spaced apart in UMAP space so the scatter reads well). On *network
failure only* (not on a real 404 from a live backend), fall back to the fixture the way
`getAuthorCards()` falls back to `AUTHORS`. Keep the fallback in the lib layer, never in
components, and log the decision. Fixture values are presentation seed data, not faked
claims about generation quality — the §8.6 honesty rule concerns fit scores.

---

## 5. Binding rules (from CLAUDE.md + design-system §7)

- Read every file before editing it. Never hand-edit `components/ui/*` (generated shadcn).
- Tokens only — no raw hex/oklch in TSX; author styling via `data-voice`/chart tokens,
  never `if (author === "poe")` color logic.
- Discriminated unions for variant props; no `as any`, no `@ts-ignore`.
- Files < 500 lines. No new npm packages. No new routes. Mobile out of scope
  (degrade gracefully: the `md:grid-cols-2` collapses to one column).
- `prefers-reduced-motion` must disable the fade-rise reveal (already handled by the
  keyframe classes in `globals.css` — just use the existing classes).
- Dark mode must work with zero component changes (it will, if you only use tokens —
  verify visually with the header ThemeToggle).

## 6. Definition of done

1. `npm run build` and `npm run lint` pass clean from `frontend/`.
2. `/author/austen`, `/author/dickens`, `/author/poe` each render the panel with the
   author's own chart color; an unknown-but-listed author falls back to the brand token.
3. All four panel states reachable and visually correct in **both** light and dark mode
   (kill the backend to see fixtures/error paths; empty state via a
   `has_style_profile: false` author).
4. Radar values visibly differ per author (fixtures must not be three identical shapes).
5. No hardcoded strings in JSX (`grep -rn '"' src/components/StyleDna*` sanity check);
   `en.ts` still `as const`.
6. Design-system inventory + decision log updated in the same PR.
7. Conventional commit(s): `feat(front): add style dna panel with radar and umap scatter`
   — branch `feat/front-style-dna`, PR references issue #43 ("Closes #43").

## 7. Out of scope (do not build)

- `distinctive_vocab` chips in the panel (already rendered in the generation columns via
  `DistinctiveVocabHighlight`; a panel treatment is a separate decision).
- `PromptComposer` / generation wiring (Sprint 2, separate issue).
- Any backend work; per-chunk scatter points (contract gap — flag, don't fake).
- Polling `style-profile` after document upload (belongs to the AddAuthorCard follow-up).

    tokens / sentences), the six radar axis labels, radar aria-label + caption, scatter
    caption (e.g. "Semantic map — each point is an author's corpus centroid; the ring
    shows dispersion"), loading status, empty title + body, error message, retry, and a
    `computedAt: (date: string) => ...` interpolation. Keep `as const` and the `EN`
    type export intact. **Zero hardcoded English in JSX.**

11. **`docs/design-system.md`** — same PR: mark `StyleDnaPanel`, `StyleRadarChart`,
    `StyleScatter2D`, `MetricChip`, `EmptyState` as ✅ built in the §7 inventory, and add
    decision-log rows for (a) the six radar axes + normalization domains, (b) the
    centroid-only scatter (contract limitation), (c) the fixture fallback below if you
    implement it.

---

## 4. Demo-safety (backend is not there yet)

The backend currently implements only `GET /api/authors` and `/health` — the
style-profile endpoint will 404/refuse for now. Follow the established
API-with-seed-fallback decision: add `src/lib/fixtures/style-profiles.ts` with **typed
`StyleProfile` fixtures for the three seed authors** (plausible, clearly-commented
values; centroids spaced apart in UMAP space so the scatter reads well). On *network
failure only* (not on a real 404 from a live backend), fall back to the fixture the way
`getAuthorCards()` falls back to `AUTHORS`. Keep the fallback in the lib layer, never in
components, and log the decision. Fixture values are presentation seed data, not faked
claims about generation quality — the §8.6 honesty rule concerns fit scores.

---

## 5. Binding rules (from CLAUDE.md + design-system §7)

- Read every file before editing it. Never hand-edit `components/ui/*` (generated shadcn).
- Tokens only — no raw hex/oklch in TSX; author styling via `data-voice`/chart tokens,
  never `if (author === "poe")` color logic.
- Discriminated unions for variant props; no `as any`, no `@ts-ignore`.
- Files < 500 lines. No new npm packages. No new routes. Mobile out of scope
  (degrade gracefully: the `md:grid-cols-2` collapses to one column).
- `prefers-reduced-motion` must disable the fade-rise reveal (already handled by the
  keyframe classes in `globals.css` — just use the existing classes).
- Dark mode must work with zero component changes (it will, if you only use tokens —
  verify visually with the header ThemeToggle).

## 6. Definition of done

1. `npm run build` and `npm run lint` pass clean from `frontend/`.
2. `/author/austen`, `/author/dickens`, `/author/poe` each render the panel with the
   author's own chart color; an unknown-but-listed author falls back to the brand token.
3. All four panel states reachable and visually correct in **both** light and dark mode
   (kill the backend to see fixtures/error paths; empty state via a
   `has_style_profile: false` author).
4. Radar values visibly differ per author (fixtures must not be three identical shapes).
5. No hardcoded strings in JSX (`grep -rn '"' src/components/StyleDna*` sanity check);
   `en.ts` still `as const`.
6. Design-system inventory + decision log updated in the same PR.
7. Conventional commit(s): `feat(front): add style dna panel with radar and umap scatter`
   — branch `feat/front-style-dna`, PR references issue #43 ("Closes #43").

## 7. Out of scope (do not build)

- `distinctive_vocab` chips in the panel (already rendered in the generation columns via
  `DistinctiveVocabHighlight`; a panel treatment is a separate decision).
- `PromptComposer` / generation wiring (Sprint 2, separate issue).
- Any backend work; per-chunk scatter points (contract gap — flag, don't fake).
- Polling `style-profile` after document upload (belongs to the AddAuthorCard follow-up).

# glob — file search
Use `glob` to find files by name pattern (e.g. "**/*.ts", "src/**/*.test.ts"). Prefer this over `list_files` when looking for specific files across the project.
Only fall back to `list_files` when you need to browse a single directory's structure.

# grep — content search
Use `grep` to find code by content using regex patterns. Prefer this over `list_files` + `read_file` when searching for specific code, symbols, or patterns.
Use `read_file` only after grep tells you which file and lines to look at.

# Subagents
You have access to `spawn_subagent` which creates an independent agent with its own context.

**Default: do the work yourself.** Most tasks are faster and cheaper without a subagent.
Only consider a subagent when ALL of these apply:
- The task is clearly self-contained and only needs a summary back
- It would add significant irrelevant content to your context
- You cannot accomplish it with 1-2 direct tool calls

Available types:
- "explore": Explore codebase (read tools, explorer model)
- "general": Full tools, default model

**fork_context**: Set to true when the subagent needs to understand prior conversation — e.g., decisions made, constraints discovered, or user preferences expressed earlier.
Leave false (default) when the description alone is sufficient context.

Do NOT use subagents for:
- Simple file reads, searches, or single-tool operations
- Tasks where you already have relevant context
- Quick lookups that would take fewer turns done directly

You have access to workflows that provide specialized multi-step processes for specific tasks. Review the available workflows below. When a user's request matches a workflow's purpose, use the start_workflow tool. Run the workflow only if you are sure the workflow description will provide appropriate assistance. When suggesting workflows to users, provide the title and description, never the internal ID.
Available workflows:
* create_pr_workflow: Create Pull Request - Generates description from git diff using PR templates, allows review and editing, then creates the pull request on GitHub.

# AutorIA — Implementation Prompt: Issue #43 "Style DNA" screen (radar + 2D scatter + metrics)

> **For:** Bob (implementation agent)
> **Repo:** autorIA · **Branch:** create `feat/front-style-dna` from `test/ui-design`
> **Milestone:** Sprint 1 — Foundation + StyleProfile · **Labels:** frontend, size:L
> **Issue text:** "Create the Style DNA visualization page. Render a Recharts Radar chart for
> lexical/syntactic metrics and a 2D Scatter plot for the semantic UMAP cluster."

---

## 0. Mission

Turn the placeholder on `/author/[id]` ("Full studio coming soon.") into the **Style DNA
panel**: a collapsible panel showing (a) corpus **MetricChips**, (b) a **Recharts Radar**
of six normalized style metrics, and (c) a **2D scatter** of the semantic UMAP space with
the selected author's cluster highlighted. Everything must follow `docs/design-system.md`
(the binding design guide), `docs/api_contract.yaml` (the locked API contract), and the
project conventions already visible in `frontend/src/components/`.

**Do not create new routes.** The app has exactly three routes (`/`, `/author/[id]`,
`/verify`). The Style DNA panel mounts inside the existing `/author/[id]` page.

---

## 1. Required reading (in this order, before writing any code)

1. `docs/design-system.md` — §2.4 (author palette + `--voice` indirection + `--chart-*`
   mapping), §3 (type scale recipes), §5 (motion), §7 (component inventory + the 7 rules
   for adding a component), §8 (5-second contrast playbook), §9 (decision log).
2. `docs/api_contract.yaml` — `GET /api/authors/{author_id}/style-profile` and the
   `StyleProfile`, `CorpusStats`, `LexicalFeatures`, `SyntacticFeatures`,
   `StylisticFeatures`, `DistinctiveTerm`, `EmbeddingUmap2d` schemas.
3. Reference implementations (copy their patterns exactly):
   - `frontend/src/components/FitScoreBar.tsx` — discriminated props, single source of
     truth for thresholds, AA-safe class maps.
   - `frontend/src/components/AuthorColumn.tsx` — the canonical async-state pattern
     (loading skeleton + status text / error + retry / success) and `data-voice` usage.
   - `frontend/src/lib/api.ts` — typed fetch client (`parseOrThrow`, `API_BASE`) and the
     CONTRACT GAP comment style.
   - `frontend/src/lib/authors.ts` — the demo-safe API-with-seed-fallback pattern
     (see decision log entry "API-with-seed-fallback").
4. `frontend/src/lib/i18n/en.ts` — string conventions (`as const`, typed interpolation
   functions, grouped by screen).

---

## 2. Contract facts you must honor

`GET /api/authors/{author_id}/style-profile` returns `StyleProfile` v1.0:

| Field | Shape | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `schema_version` | `"1.0"` const | |
| `author_id` | string | |
| `computed_at` | ISO date-time string | |
| `corpus_stats` | `{ n_documents, n_tokens, n_sentences }` (ints ≥ 0) | → MetricChips |
| `lexical` | `{ mattr_500, avg_word_length, hapax_ratio }` (numbers) | → radar axes |
| `syntactic` | `{ avg_sentence_length_tokens, std_sentence_length_tokens, subordination_ratio, passive_voice_ratio, noun_to_verb_ratio }` | → radar axes |
| `stylistic` | `{ punct_distribution: Record<string, number>, pos_distribution: Record<string, number>, dialogue_ratio, first_person_ratio }` | → radar axis (dialogue) |
| `distinctive_vocab` | `DistinctiveTerm[]` (already typed in `lib/types.ts`) | NOT rendered in this issue |
| `semantic_centroid` | `number[]` (768 floats) | NOT rendered — never ship it to a chart |
| `embedding_umap_2d` | `{ centroid: [number, number], spread: number }` | → scatter |

`404` means **author unknown OR StyleProfile not yet computed** — that is an *empty*
state ("not computed yet"), never a destructive error.

⚠️ **Scatter data limitation (do not work around it by faking data):** the contract
exposes only **one centroid + one spread per author** — there are no per-chunk points.
The scatter therefore plots **one point per author with a computed profile** (fetch the
author list, then fetch each author's profile in parallel, silently skipping 404s), with
the selected author's point in its voice color and a translucent dispersion ring scaled
by `spread`; all other authors render muted. This matches design-system §7
("selected author's cluster in its voice color, others muted") and the honesty rule
(§8.6). If you believe per-chunk points are needed, flag it as a contract gap in the
decision log — do not invent points.

---

## 3. Deliverables (files)

All components: `frontend/src/components/PascalCase.tsx`, one component per file,
< 500 lines, no `as any`, no new dependencies (Recharts `^3.9.2` and lucide-react are
already installed — note the **Recharts v3** API).

1. **`src/lib/types.ts`** — add mirrors of the contract, exactly named:
   `CorpusStats`, `LexicalFeatures`, `SyntacticFeatures`, `StylisticFeatures`,
   `EmbeddingUmap2d`, `StyleProfile`. JSDoc each with the contract reference, matching
   the existing file's style. `DistinctiveTerm` already exists — reuse it.

2. **`src/lib/api.ts`** — add `getStyleProfile(authorId: string): Promise<StyleProfile>`
   (GET, `cache: "no-store"`, via `parseOrThrow`). Throw a distinguishable error for 404
   (e.g. a small `NotFoundError` class or a typed result) so the panel can separate
   *not-computed* (empty state) from *server/network failure* (error state + retry).

3. **`src/lib/style-dna.ts`** (new, pure logic — no JSX):
   - `RADAR_AXES`: the single source of truth for the six radar axes. Each entry:
     `{ key, labelKey, domain: [min, max], select: (p: StyleProfile) => number }`.
     Axes (lexical/syntactic/stylistic mix, per issue):
     | Axis | Source field | Normalization domain |
     |---|---|---|
     | Vocabulary richness | `lexical.mattr_500` | `[0, 1]` (native) |
     | Rare words | `lexical.hapax_ratio` | `[0, 1]` (native) |
     | Word length | `lexical.avg_word_length` | `[3, 8]` chars |
     | Sentence length | `syntactic.avg_sentence_length_tokens` | `[5, 40]` tokens |
     | Subordination | `syntactic.subordination_ratio` | `[0, 1]` (native) |
     | Dialogue | `stylistic.dialogue_ratio` | `[0, 1]` (native) |
   - `normalizeAxis(value, domain)`: linear map to `[0, 1]`, clamped. Document each
     non-native domain with a comment (these are honest presentation scalings, not data
     changes — like `getFitBand`, thresholds live in exactly one place).
   - `CHART_COLOR_BY_AUTHOR`: `austen → "var(--chart-1)"`, `dickens → "var(--chart-2)"`,
     `poe → "var(--chart-3)"`, fallback (unknown/uploaded author) → `"var(--chart-4)"`;
     muted/other series → `"var(--chart-5)"`. **Recharts colors come only from these five
     tokens** (design-system §2.4) — pass them as `stroke`/`fill` strings; CSS variables
     resolve fine in SVG attributes, and they adapt to dark mode for free.

4. **`src/components/MetricChip.tsx`** — tiny presentational component:
   `font-mono tabular-nums` value + `text-xs text-muted-foreground` sans label
   (design-system §3 "Metric" + "Caption" recipes). Used for `n_documents`, `n_tokens`
   (format with `toLocaleString("en-US")`), `n_sentences`.

5. **`src/components/EmptyState.tsx`** — generic, reusable (it's in the Sprint 1
   inventory for *all* screens): lucide icon + one-line message + optional action
   (`ReactNode`). No async logic.

6. **`src/components/StyleRadarChart.tsx`** (`"use client"`) — Recharts
   `RadarChart` inside `ResponsiveContainer` at a fixed height (e.g. `h-72`); six axes
   from `RADAR_AXES`, values pre-normalized to `[0, 1]`, `domain={[0, 1]}`, radius axis
   ticks hidden or minimal. Series stroke/fill = the selected author's chart token,
   fill at low opacity (~0.25). Grid/axis text uses `var(--border)` /
   `var(--muted-foreground)`. Axis labels from `en.ts`. Container gets `role="img"` +
   a descriptive `aria-label` from `en.ts`; the MetricChips + a visually rendered
   axis-value list (or the tooltip) keep the data accessible as text.

7. **`src/components/StyleScatter2D.tsx`** (`"use client"`) — Recharts `ScatterChart`:
   one `Scatter` series per author profile provided via props
   (`{ authorId, authorName, point: [x, y], spread, selected }[]`). Selected author:
   voice chart token + dispersion ring (SVG circle sized by `spread`, ~15% opacity
   fill); others: `var(--chart-5)` muted dots. Both X and Y axes hidden (UMAP axes are
   meaningless to users — show a caption from `en.ts` explaining the map instead).
   Label each point with the author name (small `text-xs` label or Recharts
   `LabelList`) so color is never the only signal (a11y floor, design-system §7 rule 7).

8. **`src/components/StyleDnaPanel.tsx`** (`"use client"`) — the wrapper that **owns all
   fetch states** (design-system §7 inventory):
   - Props: `{ authorId: string; authorName: string }` — no fetching in the server page
     beyond what already exists.
   - On mount (and on Retry), fetch the selected author's profile **and** the comparison
     set for the scatter (author list → parallel `getStyleProfile`, skip failures).
     The panel renders as soon as the selected author's profile resolves; comparison
     points may stream in after (single state update is fine — no waterfall spinners).
   - **States (all four, no infinite spinner):**
     - `loading` — pulsing muted skeleton bars + status text (copy `AuthorColumn`'s
       skeleton pattern), plus a timeout guard (~10 s → error state with timeout copy).
     - `empty` — 404 / `has_style_profile === false` → `EmptyState` with `Feather`
       icon and "not yet computed" copy (this is *neutral*, not destructive — a freshly
       uploaded author lands here while the pipeline runs).
     - `error` — network/5xx → message + Retry `Button` (reuse the `StateNotice`
       pattern from `AuthorColumn`; `TriangleAlert`, destructive tone).
     - `ready` — layout below.
   - **Collapsible:** panel header row = section label ("Style DNA", design-system §3
     "Section" recipe) + `ChevronDown` toggle (rotate 180° with
     `transition-transform duration-200`), `aria-expanded` + `aria-controls` on the
     button. Default **expanded**. Collapse hides content only (conditional render or
     `hidden` — never animate height, design-system §5).
   - **Ready layout (desktop-first 1280, inside the existing `max-w-5xl` shell):**
     `Card` with `shadow-paper`; header row of MetricChips + `computed_at` caption
     (`font-mono text-xs text-muted-foreground`, format date only); below, a
     two-column grid `md:grid-cols-2 gap-6`: radar left, scatter right, each with a
     §3 "Section" label and a one-line caption from `en.ts`. Content fades in with
     `.animate-fade-rise`.

9. **`src/app/author/[id]/page.tsx`** — replace the `comingSoon` placeholder with
   `<StyleDnaPanel authorId={author.id} authorName={author.name} />`. Keep the
   existing `data-voice={author.id}` wrapper, back link, and serif `text-4xl` h1
   untouched. Remove `authorDetail.comingSoon` from `en.ts` only if nothing else uses it.

10. **`src/lib/i18n/en.ts`** — add a `styleDna` group. Every visible string, including:
    section title ("Style DNA"), collapse toggle aria-label, metric labels (documents /
    tokens / sentences), the six radar axis labels, radar aria-label + caption, scatter
    caption (e.g. "Semantic map — each point is an author's corpus centroid; the ring
    shows dispersion"), loading status, empty title + body, error message, retry, and a
    `computedAt: (date: string) => ...` interpolation. Keep `as const` and the `EN`
    type export intact. **Zero hardcoded English in JSX.**

11. **`docs/design-system.md`** — same PR: mark `StyleDnaPanel`, `StyleRadarChart`,
    `StyleScatter2D`, `MetricChip`, `EmptyState` as ✅ built in the §7 inventory, and add
    decision-log rows for (a) the six radar axes + normalization domains, (b) the
    centroid-only scatter (contract limitation), (c) the fixture fallback below if you
    implement it.

---

## 4. Demo-safety (backend is not there yet)

The backend currently implements only `GET /api/authors` and `/health` — the
style-profile endpoint will 404/refuse for now. Follow the established
API-with-seed-fallback decision: add `src/lib/fixtures/style-profiles.ts` with **typed
`StyleProfile` fixtures for the three seed authors** (plausible, clearly-commented
values; centroids spaced apart in UMAP space so the scatter reads well). On *network
failure only* (not on a real 404 from a live backend), fall back to the fixture the way
`getAuthorCards()` falls back to `AUTHORS`. Keep the fallback in the lib layer, never in
components, and log the decision. Fixture values are presentation seed data, not faked
claims about generation quality — the §8.6 honesty rule concerns fit scores.

---

## 5. Binding rules (from CLAUDE.md + design-system §7)

- Read every file before editing it. Never hand-edit `components/ui/*` (generated shadcn).
- Tokens only — no raw hex/oklch in TSX; author styling via `data-voice`/chart tokens,
  never `if (author === "poe")` color logic.
- Discriminated unions for variant props; no `as any`, no `@ts-ignore`.
- Files < 500 lines. No new npm packages. No new routes. Mobile out of scope
  (degrade gracefully: the `md:grid-cols-2` collapses to one column).
- `prefers-reduced-motion` must disable the fade-rise reveal (already handled by the
  keyframe classes in `globals.css` — just use the existing classes).
- Dark mode must work with zero component changes (it will, if you only use tokens —
  verify visually with the header ThemeToggle).

## 6. Definition of done

1. `npm run build` and `npm run lint` pass clean from `frontend/`.
2. `/author/austen`, `/author/dickens`, `/author/poe` each render the panel with the
   author's own chart color; an unknown-but-listed author falls back to the brand token.
3. All four panel states reachable and visually correct in **both** light and dark mode
   (kill the backend to see fixtures/error paths; empty state via a
   `has_style_profile: false` author).
4. Radar values visibly differ per author (fixtures must not be three identical shapes).
5. No hardcoded strings in JSX (`grep -rn '"' src/components/StyleDna*` sanity check);
   `en.ts` still `as const`.
6. Design-system inventory + decision log updated in the same PR.
7. Conventional commit(s): `feat(front): add style dna panel with radar and umap scatter`
   — branch `feat/front-style-dna`, PR references issue #43 ("Closes #43").

## 7. Out of scope (do not build)

- `distinctive_vocab` chips in the panel (already rendered in the generation columns via
  `DistinctiveVocabHighlight`; a panel treatment is a separate decision).
- `PromptComposer` / generation wiring (Sprint 2, separate issue).
- Any backend work; per-chunk scatter points (contract gap — flag, don't fake).
- Polling `style-profile` after document upload (belongs to the AddAuthorCard follow-up).
</user_query>

   a descriptive `aria-label` from `en.ts`; the MetricChips + a visually rendered
   axis-value list (or the tooltip) keep the data accessible as text.

7. **`src/components/StyleScatter2D.tsx`** (`"use client"`) — Recharts `ScatterChart`:
   one `Scatter` series per author profile provided via props
   (`{ authorId, authorName, point: [x, y], spread, selected }[]`). Selected author:
   voice chart token + dispersion ring (SVG circle sized by `spread`, ~15% opacity
   fill); others: `var(--chart-5)` muted dots. Both X and Y axes hidden (UMAP axes are
   meaningless to users — show a caption from `en.ts` explaining the map instead).
   Label each point with the author name (small `text-xs` label or Recharts
   `LabelList`) so color is never the only signal (a11y floor, design-system §7 rule 7).

8. **`src/components/StyleDnaPanel.tsx`** (`"use client"`) — the wrapper that **owns all
   fetch states** (design-system §7 inventory):
   - Props: `{ authorId: string; authorName: string }` — no fetching in the server page
     beyond what already exists.
   - On mount (and on Retry), fetch the selected author's profile **and** the comparison
     set for the scatter (author list → parallel `getStyleProfile`, skip failures).
     The panel renders as soon as the selected author's profile resolves; comparison
     points may stream in after (single state update is fine — no waterfall spinners).
   - **States (all four, no infinite spinner):**
     - `loading` — pulsing muted skeleton bars + status text (copy `AuthorColumn`'s
       skeleton pattern), plus a timeout guard (~10 s → error state with timeout copy).
     - `empty` — 404 / `has_style_profile === false` → `EmptyState` with `Feather`
       icon and "not yet computed" copy (this is *neutral*, not destructive — a freshly
       uploaded author lands here while the pipeline runs).
     - `error` — network/5xx → message + Retry `Button` (reuse the `StateNotice`
       pattern from `AuthorColumn`; `TriangleAlert`, destructive tone).
     - `ready` — layout below.
   - **Collapsible:** panel header row = section label ("Style DNA", design-system §3
     "Section" recipe) + `ChevronDown` toggle (rotate 180° with
     `transition-transform duration-200`), `aria-expanded` + `aria-controls` on the
     button. Default **expanded**. Collapse hides content only (conditional render or
     `hidden` — never animate height, design-system §5).
   - **Ready layout (desktop-first 1280, inside the existing `max-w-5xl` shell):**
     `Card` with `shadow-paper`; header row of MetricChips + `computed_at` caption
     (`font-mono text-xs text-muted-foreground`, format date only); below, a
     two-column grid `md:grid-cols-2 gap-6`: radar left, scatter right, each with a
     §3 "Section" label and a one-line caption from `en.ts`. Content fades in with
     `.animate-fade-rise`.

9. **`src/app/author/[id]/page.tsx`** — replace the `comingSoon` placeholder with
   `<StyleDnaPanel authorId={author.id} authorName={author.name} />`. Keep the
   existing `data-voice={author.id}` wrapper, back link, and serif `text-4xl` h1
   untouched. Remove `authorDetail.comingSoon` from `en.ts` only if nothing else uses it.

10. **`src/lib/i18n/en.ts`** — add a `styleDna` group. Every visible string, including:
    section title ("Style DNA"), collapse toggle aria-label, metric labels (documents /
    tokens / sentences), the six radar axis labels, radar aria-label + caption, scatter
    caption (e.g. "Semantic map — each point is an author's corpus centroid; the ring
    shows dispersion"), loading status, empty title + body, error message, retry, and a
    `computedAt: (date: string) => ...` interpolation. Keep `as const` and the `EN`
    type export intact. **Zero hardcoded English in JSX.**

11. **`docs/design-system.md`** — same PR: mark `StyleDnaPanel`, `StyleRadarChart`,
    `StyleScatter2D`, `MetricChip`, `EmptyState` as ✅ built in the §7 inventory, and add
    decision-log rows for (a) the six radar axes + normalization domains, (b) the
    centroid-only scatter (contract limitation), (c) the fixture fallback below if you
    implement it.

---

## 4. Demo-safety (backend is not there yet)

The backend currently implements only `GET /api/authors` and `/health` — the
style-profile endpoint will 404/refuse for now. Follow the established
API-with-seed-fallback decision: add `src/lib/fixtures/style-profiles.ts` with **typed
`StyleProfile` fixtures for the three seed authors** (plausible, clearly-commented
values; centroids spaced apart in UMAP space so the scatter reads well). On *network
failure only* (not on a real 404 from a live backend), fall back to the fixture the way
`getAuthorCards()` falls back to `AUTHORS`. Keep the fallback in the lib layer, never in
components, and log the decision. Fixture values are presentation seed data, not faked
claims about generation quality — the §8.6 honesty rule concerns fit scores.

---

## 5. Binding rules (from CLAUDE.md + design-system §7)

- Read every file before editing it. Never hand-edit `components/ui/*` (generated shadcn).
- Tokens only — no raw hex/oklch in TSX; author styling via `data-voice`/chart tokens,
  never `if (author === "poe")` color logic.
- Discriminated unions for variant props; no `as any`, no `@ts-ignore`.
- Files < 500 lines. No new npm packages. No new routes. Mobile out of scope
  (degrade gracefully: the `md:grid-cols-2` collapses to one column).
- `prefers-reduced-motion` must disable the fade-rise reveal (already handled by the
  keyframe classes in `globals.css` — just use the existing classes).
- Dark mode must work with zero component changes (it will, if you only use tokens —
  verify visually with the header ThemeToggle).

## 6. Definition of done

1. `npm run build` and `npm run lint` pass clean from `frontend/`.
2. `/author/austen`, `/author/dickens`, `/author/poe` each render the panel with the
   author's own chart color; an unknown-but-listed author falls back to the brand token.
3. All four panel states reachable and visually correct in **both** light and dark mode
   (kill the backend to see fixtures/error paths; empty state via a
   `has_style_profile: false` author).
4. Radar values visibly differ per author (fixtures must not be three identical shapes).
5. No hardcoded strings in JSX (`grep -rn '"' src/components/StyleDna*` sanity check);
   `en.ts` still `as const`.
6. Design-system inventory + decision log updated in the same PR.
7. Conventional commit(s): `feat(front): add style dna panel with radar and umap scatter`
   — branch `feat/front-style-dna`, PR references issue #43 ("Closes #43").

## 7. Out of scope (do not build)

- `distinctive_vocab` chips in the panel (already rendered in the generation columns via
  `DistinctiveVocabHighlight`; a panel treatment is a separate decision).
- `PromptComposer` / generation wiring (Sprint 2, separate issue).
- Any backend work; per-chunk scatter points (contract gap — flag, don't fake).
- Polling `style-profile` after document upload (belongs to the AddAuthorCard follow-up).

_meta.mask:
# AutorIA — Implementation Prompt: Issue #43 "Style DNA" screen (radar + 2D scatter + metrics)

> **For:** Bob (implementation agent)
> **Repo:** autorIA · **Branch:** create `feat/front-style-dna` from `test/ui-design`
> **Milestone:** Sprint 1 — Foundation + StyleProfile · **Labels:** frontend, size:L
> **Issue text:** "Create the Style DNA visualization page. Render a Recharts Radar chart for
> lexical/syntactic metrics and a 2D Scatter plot for the semantic UMAP cluster."

---

## 0. Mission

Turn the placeholder on `/author/[id]` ("Full studio coming soon.") into the **Style DNA
panel**: a collapsible panel showing (a) corpus **MetricChips**, (b) a **Recharts Radar**
of six normalized style metrics, and (c) a **2D scatter** of the semantic UMAP space with
the selected author's cluster highlighted. Everything must follow `docs/design-system.md`
(the binding design guide), `docs/api_contract.yaml` (the locked API contract), and the
project conventions already visible in `frontend/src/components/`.

**Do not create new routes.** The app has exactly three routes (`/`, `/author/[id]`,
`/verify`). The Style DNA panel mounts inside the existing `/author/[id]` page.

---

## 1. Required reading (in this order, before writing any code)

1. `docs/design-system.md` — §2.4 (author palette + `--voice` indirection + `--chart-*`
   mapping), §3 (type scale recipes), §5 (motion), §7 (component inventory + the 7 rules
   for adding a component), §8 (5-second contrast playbook), §9 (decision log).
2. `docs/api_contract.yaml` — `GET /api/authors/{author_id}/style-profile` and the
   `StyleProfile`, `CorpusStats`, `LexicalFeatures`, `SyntacticFeatures`,
   `StylisticFeatures`, `DistinctiveTerm`, `EmbeddingUmap2d` schemas.
3. Reference implementations (copy their patterns exactly):
   - `frontend/src/components/FitScoreBar.tsx` — discriminated props, single source of
     truth for thresholds, AA-safe class maps.
   - `frontend/src/components/AuthorColumn.tsx` — the canonical async-state pattern
     (loading skeleton + status text / error + retry / success) and `data-voice` usage.
   - `frontend/src/lib/api.ts` — typed fetch client (`parseOrThrow`, `API_BASE`) and the
     CONTRACT GAP comment style.
   - `frontend/src/lib/authors.ts` — the demo-safe API-with-seed-fallback pattern
     (see decision log entry "API-with-seed-fallback").
4. `frontend/src/lib/i18n/en.ts` — string conventions (`as const`, typed interpolation
   functions, grouped by screen).

---

## 2. Contract facts you must honor

`GET /api/authors/{author_id}/style-profile` returns `StyleProfile` v1.0:

| Field | Shape | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `schema_version` | `"1.0"` const | |
| `author_id` | string | |
| `computed_at` | ISO date-time string | |
| `corpus_stats` | `{ n_documents, n_tokens, n_sentences }` (ints ≥ 0) | → MetricChips |
| `lexical` | `{ mattr_500, avg_word_length, hapax_ratio }` (numbers) | → radar axes |
| `syntactic` | `{ avg_sentence_length_tokens, std_sentence_length_tokens, subordination_ratio, passive_voice_ratio, noun_to_verb_ratio }` | → radar axes |
| `stylistic` | `{ punct_distribution: Record<string, number>, pos_distribution: Record<string, number>, dialogue_ratio, first_person_ratio }` | → radar axis (dialogue) |
| `distinctive_vocab` | `DistinctiveTerm[]` (already typed in `lib/types.ts`) | NOT rendered in this issue |
| `semantic_centroid` | `number[]` (768 floats) | NOT rendered — never ship it to a chart |
| `embedding_umap_2d` | `{ centroid: [number, number], spread: number }` | → scatter |

`404` means **author unknown OR StyleProfile not yet computed** — that is an *empty*
state ("not computed yet"), never a destructive error.

⚠️ **Scatter data limitation (do not work around it by faking data):** the contract
exposes only **one centroid + one spread per author** — there are no per-chunk points.
The scatter therefore plots **one point per author with a computed profile** (fetch the
author list, then fetch each author's profile in parallel, silently skipping 404s), with
the selected author's point in its voice color and a translucent dispersion ring scaled
by `spread`; all other authors render muted. This matches design-system §7
("selected author's cluster in its voice color, others muted") and the honesty rule
(§8.6). If you believe per-chunk points are needed, flag it as a contract gap in the
decision log — do not invent points.

---

## 3. Deliverables (files)

All components: `frontend/src/components/PascalCase.tsx`, one component per file,
< 500 lines, no `as any`, no new dependencies (Recharts `^3.9.2` and lucide-react are
already installed — note the **Recharts v3** API).

1. **`src/lib/types.ts`** — add mirrors of the contract, exactly named:
   `CorpusStats`, `LexicalFeatures`, `SyntacticFeatures`, `StylisticFeatures`,
   `EmbeddingUmap2d`, `StyleProfile`. JSDoc each with the contract reference, matching
   the existing file's style. `DistinctiveTerm` already exists — reuse it.

2. **`src/lib/api.ts`** — add `getStyleProfile(authorId: string): Promise<StyleProfile>`
   (GET, `cache: "no-store"`, via `parseOrThrow`). Throw a distinguishable error for 404
   (e.g. a small `NotFoundError` class or a typed result) so the panel can separate
   *not-computed* (empty state) from *server/network failure* (error state + retry).

3. **`src/lib/style-dna.ts`** (new, pure logic — no JSX):
   - `RADAR_AXES`: the single source of truth for the six radar axes. Each entry:
     `{ key, labelKey, domain: [min, max], select: (p: StyleProfile) => number }`.
     Axes (lexical/syntactic/stylistic mix, per issue):
     | Axis | Source field | Normalization domain |
     |---|---|---|
     | Vocabulary richness | `lexical.mattr_500` | `[0, 1]` (native) |
     | Rare words | `lexical.hapax_ratio` | `[0, 1]` (native) |
     | Word length | `lexical.avg_word_length` | `[3, 8]` chars |
     | Sentence length | `syntactic.avg_sentence_length_tokens` | `[5, 40]` tokens |
     | Subordination | `syntactic.subordination_ratio` | `[0, 1]` (native) |
     | Dialogue | `stylistic.dialogue_ratio` | `[0, 1]` (native) |
   - `normalizeAxis(value, domain)`: linear map to `[0, 1]`, clamped. Document each
     non-native domain with a comment (these are honest presentation scalings, not data
     changes — like `getFitBand`, thresholds live in exactly one place).
   - `CHART_COLOR_BY_AUTHOR`: `austen → "var(--chart-1)"`, `dickens → "var(--chart-2)"`,
     `poe → "var(--chart-3)"`, fallback (unknown/uploaded author) → `"var(--chart-4)"`;
     muted/other series → `"var(--chart-5)"`. **Recharts colors come only from these five
     tokens** (design-system §2.4) — pass them as `stroke`/`fill` strings; CSS variables

data.content:
Contents of file docs/design-system.md:

1 | # AutorIA Design System — "The Authorship Studio"
2 | 
3 | Single source of truth for all UI work in Sprints 1–3. Extend this file and the tokens in
4 | `frontend/src/app/globals.css`; never restyle from scratch. Every color is a token, every
5 | string lives in `frontend/src/lib/i18n/en.ts`, every async surface has loading / error /
6 | empty states.
7 | 
8 | ---
9 | 
10 | ## 1. Brand & voice
11 | 
12 | **Principles**
13 | 
14 | 1. **Ink on parchment.** Warm paper surfaces, deep warm-ink text. Calm and literary, never
15 |    sterile SaaS gray.
16 | 2. **The signature is the product.** One confident sepia-bronze accent (`--primary`) marks
17 |    authorial/trusted moments; seal-green marks *verified*. Nothing else shouts.
18 | 3. **Author-coded voices.** Austen, Dickens, and Poe each own a signature hue. Anything
19 |    "in voice" — card accents, DNA clusters, the AutorIA output column, highlighted vocab —
20 |    carries that hue. This is the engine of the 5-second bar.
21 | 
22 | **Do**
23 | 
24 | - Use serif (`font-heading` / `font-serif`, Fraunces) for author names, hero headings,
25 |   passport headings, and generated literary passages.
26 | - Use `font-mono` for anything forensic: fit scores, latencies, hashes, JWS, JSON.
27 | - Pair every color signal with a word or icon (e.g. "87% Dickens-fit" next to the green
28 |   bar; a check icon inside the verified banner).
29 | - Keep interactions ≤ 250ms; reserve the 600ms reveal for the generate moment.
30 | 
31 | **Don't**
32 | 
33 | - No purple gradients, neon, glassmorphism, or emoji-as-UI.
34 | - No hardcoded hex/oklch in components — tokens only.
35 | - No new UI/animation/chart libraries without a Decision Log entry below.
36 | - No color-only meaning, ever (fit bands and verified states must read in grayscale).
37 | - No spinner without a timeout path. Watsonx can hang; the demo is recorded live.
38 | 
39 | ---
40 | 
41 | ## 2. Color system
42 | 
43 | All values are oklch and live in `globals.css` (`:root` = light, `.dark` = dark). Utilities
44 | come from the `@theme inline` mapping (`bg-voice-tint`, `text-fit-high`, `shadow-paper`…).
45 | 
46 | ### 2.1 Core semantic tokens
47 | 
48 | | Token | Light | Dark | Role |
49 | |---|---|---|---|
50 | | `--background` | `oklch(0.977 0.006 90)` | `oklch(0.185 0.01 55)` | Parchment page |
51 | | `--foreground` | `oklch(0.24 0.015 55)` | `oklch(0.945 0.008 85)` | Warm ink text |
52 | | `--card` | `oklch(0.99 0.004 95)` | `oklch(0.225 0.012 55)` | Raised paper |
53 | | `--muted` / `--muted-foreground` | `0.946` / `0.49` | `0.265` / `0.72` | Quiet surfaces / secondary text |
54 | | `--primary` | `oklch(0.44 0.085 55)` | `oklch(0.78 0.075 70)` | **Brand signature** (sepia-bronze ink) |
55 | | `--secondary`, `--accent` | warm near-parchment washes | warm near-ink washes | Chips, hovers |
56 | | `--border` / `--input` | `oklch(0.902 0.012 80)` | `oklch(1 0 0 / 12–16%)` | Hairlines |
57 | | `--ring` | `oklch(0.55 0.08 55)` | `oklch(0.68 0.06 70)` | Focus ring (always visible) |
58 | | `--destructive` | `oklch(0.52 0.19 27)` | `oklch(0.66 0.17 25)` | Errors / invalid passport |
59 | 
60 | ### 2.2 State colors
61 | 
62 | | Token | Light | Dark | Use |
63 | |---|---|---|---|
64 | | `--success` | `oklch(0.50 0.11 155)` | `oklch(0.70 0.12 155)` | Seal-green: VERIFIED banner, stamp |
65 | | `--success-tint` | `oklch(0.955 0.03 155)` | `oklch(0.30 0.05 155)` | Verified banner background |
66 | | `--warning` | `oklch(0.62 0.12 75)` | `oklch(0.75 0.12 80)` | Timeouts, degraded service (icon/border only) |
67 | | `--warning-tint` + `--warning-foreground` | wash + dark amber text | | Warning surfaces |
68 | 
69 | ### 2.3 Fit-score scale (used by `FitScoreBar`)
70 | 
71 | Perceptually even: lightness stays ~0.56–0.72 (light) / 0.68–0.78 (dark) so no stop
72 | "glows" louder than its meaning. **Low fit is neutral gray, not red** — a low score means
73 | *generic*, not *broken*; red is reserved for real errors.
74 | 
75 | | Band | Score | Token | Light | Meaning |
76 | |---|---|---|---|---|
77 | | generic | 0–39 | `--fit-generic` | `oklch(0.60 0.02 75)` | Anonymous, model-default prose |
78 | | weak | 40–54 | `--fit-weak` | `oklch(0.60 0.135 40)` | Faint voice (clay) |
79 | | mid | 55–69 | `--fit-mid` | `oklch(0.72 0.13 85)` | Partial voice (amber) |
80 | | good | 70–84 | `--fit-good` | `oklch(0.66 0.125 130)` | Clear voice (moss) |
81 | | high | 85–100 | `--fit-high` | `oklch(0.56 0.135 152)` | Unmistakable voice (seal green) |
82 | 
83 | `--fit-track` is the empty-bar background. Band thresholds live in ONE place:
84 | `getFitBand()` in `FitScoreBar.tsx` — import it, never re-implement.
85 | 
86 | **Text rule:** only `--fit-high` and `--fit-generic`/`muted-foreground` are approved as
87 | *text* colors (they pass AA on parchment). `weak/mid/good` are fill-only — captions next
88 | to them use `text-foreground`/`text-muted-foreground`.
89 | 
90 | ### 2.4 Author signature palette
91 | 
92 | Each author has `--author-<id>` (solid), `--author-<id>-tint` (wash), `--author-<id>-on`
93 | (text on solid). Light-mode solids are all L ≤ 0.52 so they pass AA as text on parchment.
94 | 
95 | | Author | Hue story | Solid (light) | Solid (dark) |
96 | |---|---|---|---|
97 | | Austen | Regency rose / claret | `oklch(0.52 0.13 10)` | `oklch(0.72 0.11 10)` |
98 | | Dickens | Victorian deep teal | `oklch(0.45 0.085 200)` | `oklch(0.73 0.09 200)` |
99 | | Poe | Gothic oxblood | `oklch(0.41 0.135 25)` | `oklch(0.68 0.13 25)` |
100 | 
101 | **The `--voice` indirection (how components stay author-agnostic):** components never name
102 | an author. They use `bg-voice`, `bg-voice-tint`, `text-voice`, `text-voice-on`; an ancestor
103 | sets `data-voice="dickens"` and CSS maps `--voice → --author-dickens` etc. No `data-voice`
104 | (or an unknown/live-uploaded author) falls back to the brand signature — new authors work
105 | instantly with zero component changes. **To add an author:** add three `--author-*` vars in
106 | `:root` + `.dark` and one `[data-voice="<id>"]` block. Nothing else.
107 | 
108 | Recharts mapping: `--chart-1` = Austen, `--chart-2` = Dickens, `--chart-3` = Poe,
109 | `--chart-4` = brand, `--chart-5` = neutral. Radar/scatter series colors come only from
110 | these.
111 | 
112 | ### 2.5 Contrast notes (AA)
113 | 
114 | - `foreground` on `background`: ~12:1 both modes — safe everywhere.
115 | - `muted-foreground` on `background`: ≥ 6:1 — safe for captions.
116 | - Author solids as text on parchment: ≥ 4.6:1 — safe (that's why L ≤ 0.52).
117 | - `text-voice` on `bg-voice-tint`: ≥ 4.5:1 for all three authors — safe for chips/marks.
118 | - **Borderline / forbidden:** `--warning` and `--fit-mid`/`--fit-good`/`--fit-weak` as
119 |   text on parchment (≈ 2–3:1) — fills, borders, icons only. White text on `--warning` —
120 |   never; use `--warning-foreground` on `--warning-tint`.
121 | 
122 | ---
123 | 
124 | ## 3. Typography
125 | 
126 | | Role | Font | Token / class | Usage |
127 | |---|---|---|---|
128 | | Editorial display | **Fraunces** (next/font, self-hosted) | `font-heading`, `font-display` | Author names, page titles, passport headings, verified banner title |
129 | | Literary body | Fraunces | `font-serif` | Generated passages only |
130 | | UI / body | Geist Sans | `font-sans` (default) | Everything else |
131 | | Forensic | Geist Mono | `font-mono` | Scores, latency, hashes, JWS, JSON |
132 | 
133 | **Type scale** (Tailwind utilities; don't invent sizes):
134 | 
135 | | Step | Class recipe | Use |
136 | |---|---|---|
137 | | Display | `font-heading text-4xl font-semibold tracking-tight` | Studio hero (author name) |
138 | | Title | `font-heading text-3xl font-semibold tracking-tight` | Page headings |
139 | | Card title | `font-heading text-2xl font-semibold tracking-tight` | Author cards, panel titles |
140 | | Section | `text-sm font-semibold uppercase tracking-wide text-muted-foreground` | Panel section labels |
141 | | Body | `text-[0.9375rem] leading-relaxed` | UI copy |
142 | | Literary | `font-serif text-[0.9375rem] leading-7 max-w-[66ch] whitespace-pre-line` | Generated text — measure ~66ch, generous leading |
143 | | Caption | `text-xs text-muted-foreground` | Metadata, legends |
144 | | Metric | `font-mono tabular-nums` | All numbers that can change |
145 | 
146 | Generated passages keep real quotation marks from the model output; do not strip or
147 | re-typeset them.
148 | 
149 | ---
150 | 
151 | ## 4. Spacing, radius, elevation
152 | 
153 | - **Rhythm:** 4/8px grid. Card padding `p-5`/`p-6`; column gap `gap-6`; page sections
154 |   `gap-8`/`gap-10`. Shell stays `max-w-5xl px-6`, designed at 1280–1440px (desktop-first;
155 |   mobile out of scope, degrade gracefully with existing grid fallbacks).
156 | - **Radius:** existing scale off `--radius: 0.625rem` (`rounded-lg` default, `rounded-xl`
157 |   cards, `rounded-full` chips/bars). Don't add new radii.
158 | - **Elevation ("paper-lift"), 3 steps:** `shadow-paper-sm` (resting card),
159 |   `shadow-paper` (raised panel / output column), `shadow-paper-lg` (hover lift, popover).
160 |   Warm-ink shadows at ≤ 14% alpha — never heavy drops.
161 | 
162 | ---
163 | 
164 | ## 5. Motion
165 | 
166 | Tokens in `globals.css`: `--duration-fast: 150ms`, `--duration-base: 250ms`,
167 | `--duration-reveal: 600ms`, `--ease-out-soft: cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1)`.
168 | 
169 | | Moment | Recipe |
170 | |---|---|
171 | | Hover / focus | `transition-*` at 150–200ms; cards lift `-translate-y-0.5` + `shadow-paper-lg` |
172 | | Generate reveal | `.animate-fade-rise` on the passage (250ms fade + 8px rise) |
173 | | Fit-bar fill | `.animate-fit-grow` — width grows 0 → score over 600ms; the two bars filling to very different lengths *is* the demo moment |
174 | | Verified stamp | `.animate-stamp-in` — 400ms scale 1.08 → 1 with a settle, like a seal pressed onto paper |
175 | 
176 | `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` disables all three keyframe classes (already in
177 | `globals.css`). Never animate layout (width/height of containers) — only transform,
178 | opacity, and the fit-bar fill.
179 | 
180 | ---
181 | 
182 | ## 6. Iconography
183 | 
184 | **lucide-react only** (already installed). Size `size-4` inline, `size-5` in banners.
185 | Stroke width default. Icons always accompany, never replace, a label. Canonical picks:
186 | `BadgeCheck` (verified), `ShieldAlert` (invalid), `Clock` (timeout), `TriangleAlert`
187 | (error), `Feather` (authorial/voice), `Download` (passport), `Upload` (add author),
188 | `ChevronDown` (collapsible).
189 | 
190 | ---
191 | 
192 | ## 7. Component inventory
193 | 
194 | | Component | Sprint | Screen(s) | States | Notes |
195 | |---|---|---|---|---|
196 | | AppShell / header | 1 | all | — | Sticky, `max-w-5xl`, brand serif + Art. 50 tag |
197 | | `AuthorCard` | 1 | `/` | default, hover, focus | `data-voice` accent bar, serif name |
198 | | `AuthorGrid` | 1 | `/` | populated, empty | 3-col desktop; last slot = `AddAuthorCard` |
199 | | `AddAuthorCard` | 1 ✅ | `/` | idle, editing, submitting, success, error | Dashed card → inline name + .txt/.md upload form |
200 | | `ThemeToggle` | 1 ✅ | all (header) | light, dark | CSS-only icon swap; pre-paint init script in layout |
201 | | `StyleDnaPanel` | 1 | studio | loading, error, ready, collapsed | Collapsible wrapper; owns fetch states |
202 | | `StyleRadarChart` | 1 | studio | ready | Recharts radar, 6 axes normalized [0,1], series = `--chart-*` |
203 | | `StyleScatter2D` | 1 | studio | ready | UMAP clusters; selected author's cluster in its voice color, others muted |
204 | | `MetricChip` | 1 | studio | ready | `font-mono` value + sans label |
205 | | `EmptyState` | 1 | all | — | Icon + one line + optional action |
206 | | `LoadingSkeleton` | 1 | all | — | Pulsing muted bars; pattern shown in `AuthorColumn` |
207 | | `PromptComposer` | 2 | studio | idle, submitting, disabled | Textarea + Generate (primary button) |
208 | | `AuthorColumn` | 2 ✅ | studio | loading, success, error, timeout | Built — the reference implementation |
209 | | `SideBySideOutput` | 2 ✅ | studio | delegates to columns | Built |
210 | | `FitScoreBar` | 2 ✅ | studio, verify | pending, filled | Built — exemplary; copy its patterns |
211 | | `DistinctiveVocabHighlight` | 2 ✅ | studio | — | Built — voice-tinted `<mark>` |
212 | | `PassportCard` | 2 | studio, verify | ready | Mono JSON block + `Download` action |
213 | | `VerifyForm` | 2 | `/verify` | idle, submitting, verified, invalid, error | Paste JWS / upload JSON |
214 | | `VerifiedBanner` | 2 | `/verify` | verified | `success-tint` bg, `BadgeCheck`, `.animate-stamp-in` |
215 | | `PassportErrorList` | 2 | `/verify` | invalid | Structured `VerifyError[]`, destructive styling |
216 | | Cluster-landing reveal, polish | 3 | studio | — | Motion + state audit only, no new components |
217 | 
218 | ### Rules for adding a component (keeps the system scalable)
219 | 
220 | 1. **Placement & naming:** `frontend/src/components/PascalCase.tsx`, one component per
221 |    file, < 500 lines. shadcn primitives stay in `components/ui/` (generated — don't hand-edit).
222 | 2. **Props from contracts:** consume types from `lib/types.ts` that mirror
223 |    `docs/api_contract.yaml`. Missing field → flag it and add a typed fixture; never `as any`.
224 | 3. **Tokens only:** if you need a color that has no token, add the token to `globals.css`
225 |    *and a row to §2* in the same PR. Author-specific styling goes through `data-voice`,
226 |    never `if (author === "poe")` color logic in TSX.
227 | 4. **Three states minimum** for anything async: loading (skeleton + status text), error
228 |    (message + retry), success. Timeouts get their own copy (`warning`, not `destructive`).
229 | 5. **Strings:** every visible string into `en.ts` first, grouped by screen; interpolations
230 |    are typed functions.
231 | 6. **Discriminated-union props** for variant components (see `AuthorColumn`) so invalid
232 |    combinations (an authorial column with no author) don't compile.
233 | 7. **A11y floor:** visible focus ring, AA text, `role`/`aria-*` on meters and status
234 |    regions, color always paired with a word or icon.
235 | 
236 | ---
237 | 
238 | ## 8. The 5-second contrast playbook
239 | 
240 | The devices that make vanilla vs AutorIA legible without reading a word, in priority order:
241 | 
242 | 1. **The fit-bar gap.** Two `FitScoreBar`s aligned in twin column footers: vanilla fills
243 |    ~⅓ in neutral gray, AutorIA fills ~⅞ in seal green, both animating on reveal. The gap
244 |    in *length* carries the message in grayscale; color and the big mono numbers amplify it.
245 | 2. **Named vs anonymous captions.** "34% generic" vs "87% Dickens-fit" — the words do the
246 |    work for anyone who can't parse a chart.
247 | 3. **The voice-colored column.** The AutorIA column wears the author's signature: colored
248 |    top rule, tinted label chip ("AutorIA · Dickens voice"). The vanilla column is
249 |    deliberately beige/gray with a generic chip ("Llama 3.3 · vanilla") — visually anonymous
250 |    *by design*.
251 | 4. **Highlighted signature vocabulary.** Distinctive terms glow in voice-tinted `<mark>`s
252 |    only in the AutorIA column, with a one-line legend. The vanilla side has nothing to
253 |    highlight — its blankness is the point.
254 | 5. **Cluster identity (Style DNA).** The author's UMAP cluster and radar series render in
255 |    the same signature hue as their column, so the color = author association is learned
256 |    before generation even runs. (Sprint 3: generated-output dot "lands" inside the cluster.)
257 | 6. **Honesty rule:** both columns use the same model and the real scores. The contrast is
258 |    earned by design, never faked.
259 | 
260 | ---
261 | 
262 | ## 9. Decision log
263 | 
264 | | Date | Decision | Why |
265 | |---|---|---|
266 | | 2026-07-12 | Fraunces (variable, `opsz`) as the single editorial serif via next/font | Literary title-page feel; pairs with Geist; no CDN |
267 | | 2026-07-12 | `--voice` indirection via `data-voice` attribute | Author theming with zero per-author component code; live-uploaded authors fall back to brand |
268 | | 2026-07-12 | Low fit = neutral gray, not red | Low fit means *generic*, not *error*; red stays reserved for real failures |
269 | | 2026-07-12 | Fixed `--font-sans` mapping (was circular `var(--font-sans)`) | Geist was never actually applied |
270 | | 2026-07-12 | Micro-interactions in CSS/Tailwind only; no framer-motion | Motion needs are 4 small effects; revisit only if Sprint 3 demands orchestration |
271 | | 2026-07-12 | Hand-rolled dark-mode toggle (no next-themes) | One class + localStorage + a 3-line pre-paint script; not worth a dependency |
272 | | 2026-07-12 | Author list fetches `GET /api/authors` with seed fallback | Live-uploaded authors appear; selector never renders empty if the API is down |
273 | | 2026-07-12 | **Contract gap flagged:** no create-author endpoint | Add-author flow posts to `/api/authors/{new-slug}/documents` and expects backend auto-create; contract's 404 contradicts this — resolve in contract pairing session |
274 |

export async function getStyleProfile(authorId: string): Promise<StyleProfile> {
  const res = await fetch(
    `${API_BASE}/api/authors/${encodeURIComponent(authorId)}/style-profile`,
    { cache: "no-store" },
  );
  if (res.status === 404) {

    );
  }
  return parseOrThrow<StyleProfile>(

  );
}

Index: file:///c%3A/Users/Sergi/Desktop/Repos/autorIA/frontend/src/lib/style-dna.ts
===================================================================
--- file:///c%3A/Users/Sergi/Desktop/Repos/autorIA/frontend/src/lib/style-dna.ts
+++ file:///c%3A/Users/Sergi/Desktop/Repos/autorIA/frontend/src/lib/style-dna.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,114 @@
+/**
+ * Pure logic for the Style DNA panel — no JSX.
+ * This is the single source of truth for radar axes, normalization domains,
+ * and chart color assignment (design-system.md §2.4).
+ */
+import type { StyleProfile } from "@/lib/types";
+
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+// Radar axis definitions
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+export interface RadarAxisDef {
+  /** Unique key used as dataKey in Recharts. */
+  key: string;
+  /** Key into en.styleDna.radarAxes for the axis label. */
+  labelKey: string;
+  /**
+   * The [min, max] domain used for normalization.
+   * Native [0,1] ranges need no comment; non-native ranges are annotated.
+   */
+  domain: [number, number];
+  /** Extracts the raw value from a StyleProfile. */
+  select: (p: StyleProfile) => number;
+}
+
+/**
+ * The six radar axes for the Style DNA panel.
+ * Non-native normalization domains are annotated — they are honest presentation
+ * scalings (like getFitBand thresholds), not data changes. Thresholds live here
+ * and only here; import this constant wherever an axis definition is needed.
+ */
+export const RADAR_AXES: ReadonlyArray<RadarAxisDef> = [
+  {
+    key: "vocab_richness",
+    labelKey: "vocabRichness",
+    domain: [0, 1], // MATTR-500 is natively [0, 1]
+    select: (p) => p.lexical.mattr_500,
+  },
+  {
+    key: "rare_words",
+    labelKey: "rareWords",
+    domain: [0, 1], // hapax_ratio is natively [0, 1]
+    select: (p) => p.lexical.hapax_ratio,
+  },
+  {
+    key: "word_length",
+    labelKey: "wordLength",
+    // Typical literary word length spans ~3 (short words) to ~8 (long/Latin vocabulary).
+    // Values outside this range are clamped, not hidden.
+    domain: [3, 8],
+    select: (p) => p.lexical.avg_word_length,
+  },
+  {
+    key: "sentence_length",
+    labelKey: "sentenceLength",
+    // Typical literary sentence length spans ~5 (punchy / dialogue) to ~40 (complex prose).
+    // Values outside this range are clamped, not hidden.
+    domain: [5, 40],
+    select: (p) => p.syntactic.avg_sentence_length_tokens,
+  },
+  {
+    key: "subordination",
+    labelKey: "subordination",
+    domain: [0, 1], // subordination_ratio is natively [0, 1]
+    select: (p) => p.syntactic.subordination_ratio,
+  },
+  {
+    key: "dialogue",
+    labelKey: "dialogue",
+    domain: [0, 1], // dialogue_ratio is natively [0, 1]
+    select: (p) => p.stylistic.dialogue_ratio,
+  },
+] as const;
+
+/**
+ * Linear normalization: maps `value` from `domain` to [0, 1], clamped.
+ * Used to prepare data for Recharts RadarChart with domain={[0, 1]}.
+ */
+export function normalizeAxis(value: number, domain: [number, number]): number {
+  const [min, max] = domain;
+  if (max === min) return 0;
+  return Math.max(0, Math.min(1, (value - min) / (max - min)));
+}
+
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+// Chart color assignment (design-system.md §2.4 Recharts mapping)
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+/**
+ * Returns the CSS custom-property string for the Recharts series color of a given authorId.
+ * Recharts accepts CSS variables as `stroke`/`fill` strings and they resolve correctly in SVG.
+ *
+ * chart-1 = Austen · chart-2 = Dickens · chart-3 = Poe
+ * chart-4 = brand (unknown / live-uploaded author)
+ * chart-5 = neutral muted (all other / comparison authors)
+ *
+ * NEVER use if-author logic in components — call this function from lib only.
+ */
+export function chartColorForAuthor(
+  authorId: string,
+  role: "selected" | "other" = "other",
+): string {
+  if (role === "other") return "var(--chart-5)";
+  switch (authorId) {
+    case "austen":
+      return "var(--chart-1)";
+    case "dickens":
+      return "var(--chart-2)";
+    case "poe":
+      return "var(--chart-3)";
+    default:
+      return "var(--chart-4)";
+  }
+}

+/**
+ * Typed StyleProfile fixtures for the three seed authors.
+ * Used as a demo-safe fallback when the backend style-profile endpoint is
+ * unavailable (network error / not yet implemented). This mirrors the
+ * API-with-seed-fallback pattern in lib/authors.ts.
+ *
+ * IMPORTANT: These are presentation seed data for development and demos only.
+ * - They are NOT used when the live API returns a real 404 (not-computed state).
+ * - They are ONLY substituted on network failure (fetch throws / non-404 HTTP error).
+ * - Values are plausible but representative of each author's style — they are
+ *   intentionally distinct so the radar chart reads clearly.
+ * - The §8.6 honesty rule does not apply here (this is not a fit score).
+ *
+ * Centroids are spaced apart in UMAP space so the scatter plot is readable:
+ *   Austen → upper-right quadrant
+ *   Dickens → lower-left quadrant
+ *   Poe → lower-right quadrant (more solitary, high spread)
+ */
+import type { StyleProfile } from "@/lib/types";
+
+export const FIXTURE_STYLE_PROFILES: Readonly<Record<string, StyleProfile>> = {
+  austen: {
+    schema_version: "1.0",
+    author_id: "austen",
+    computed_at: "2026-07-12T10:00:00Z",
+    corpus_stats: {
+      n_documents: 3,
+      n_tokens: 412_000,
+      n_sentences: 21_800,
+    },
+    lexical: {
+      // High vocabulary richness — Austen's controlled irony uses varied word choice
+      mattr_500: 0.72,
+      // Moderate word length — elegant but not Latinate
+      avg_word_length: 4.6,
+      // Low hapax ratio — she repeats signature words across novels
+      hapax_ratio: 0.18,
+    },
+    syntactic: {
+      // Moderate sentence length — balanced, well-constructed sentences
+      avg_sentence_length_tokens: 22.0,
+      std_sentence_length_tokens: 9.5,
+      // High subordination — complex nested clauses
+      subordination_ratio: 0.41,
+      passive_voice_ratio: 0.08,
+      noun_to_verb_ratio: 1.6,
+    },
+    stylistic: {
+      punct_distribution: { ",": 0.48, ".": 0.22, ";": 0.14, ":": 0.06, "?": 0.08, "!": 0.02 },
+      pos_distribution: { NOUN: 0.22, VERB: 0.14, ADJ: 0.12, ADV: 0.08, PUNCT: 0.18 },
+      // Low dialogue ratio — social drama told through narration
+      dialogue_ratio: 0.22,
+      // Low first-person ratio — third-person narration
+      first_person_ratio: 0.04,
+    },
+    distinctive_vocab: [
+      { term: "elegance", score: 0.85 },
+      { term: "propriety", score: 0.82 },
+      { term: "sensibility", score: 0.79 },
+    ],
+    // 768 floats — not used in UI; placeholder filled with zeros
+    semantic_centroid: Array(768).fill(0),
+    embedding_umap_2d: {
+      // Upper-right: social, ordered, restrained
+      centroid: [2.8, 3.4],
+      spread: 0.6,
+    },
+  },
+
+  dickens: {
+    schema_version: "1.0",
+    author_id: "dickens",
+    computed_at: "2026-07-12T10:00:00Z",
+    corpus_stats: {
+      n_documents: 3,
+      n_tokens: 630_000,
+      n_sentences: 28_500,
+    },
+    lexical: {
+      // Moderate-high vocabulary richness — broad range of registers
+      mattr_500: 0.68,
+      // Slightly elevated — vivid, specific nouns
+      avg_word_length: 4.9,
+      // Higher hapax ratio — inventive coinages and character names
+      hapax_ratio: 0.31,
+    },
+    syntactic: {
+      // Longer sentences — sweeping Victorian prose
+      avg_sentence_length_tokens: 31.0,
+      std_sentence_length_tokens: 14.2,
+      // Moderate subordination
+      subordination_ratio: 0.35,
+      passive_voice_ratio: 0.12,
+      noun_to_verb_ratio: 1.8,
+    },
+    stylistic: {
+      punct_distribution: { ",": 0.42, ".": 0.24, ";": 0.10, ":": 0.08, "?": 0.06, "!": 0.10 },
+      pos_distribution: { NOUN: 0.24, VERB: 0.12, ADJ: 0.15, ADV: 0.10, PUNCT: 0.16 },
+      // Higher dialogue ratio — memorable character voices
+      dialogue_ratio: 0.38,
+      // Low — third-person omniscient with satirical intrusions
+      first_person_ratio: 0.06,
+    },
+    distinctive_vocab: [
+      { term: "countenance", score: 0.88 },
+      { term: "wretched", score: 0.84 },
+      { term: "miserable", score: 0.80 },
+    ],
+    semantic_centroid: Array(768).fill(0),
+    embedding_umap_2d: {
+      // Lower-left: social, expansive, energetic
+      centroid: [-2.1, -2.6],
+      spread: 0.9,
+    },
+  },
+
+  poe: {
+    schema_version: "1.0",
+    author_id: "poe",
+    computed_at: "2026-07-12T10:00:00Z",
+    corpus_stats: {
+      n_documents: 15,
+      n_tokens: 185_000,
+      n_sentences: 7_200,
+    },
+    lexical: {
+      // Lower richness per window — intense, obsessive repetition
+      mattr_500: 0.58,
+      // Longer words — Gothic Latinate vocabulary
+      avg_word_length: 5.3,
+      // High hapax ratio — idiosyncratic, rare word choices
+      hapax_ratio: 0.44,
+    },
+    syntactic: {
+      // Shorter, punchy sentences — dread through brevity
+      avg_sentence_length_tokens: 17.0,
+      std_sentence_length_tokens: 10.8,
+      // Lower subordination — direct, percussive rhythm
+      subordination_ratio: 0.24,
+      passive_voice_ratio: 0.15,
+      noun_to_verb_ratio: 1.4,
+    },
+    stylistic: {
+      punct_distribution: { ",": 0.38, ".": 0.28, ";": 0.08, ":": 0.06, "?": 0.06, "!": 0.14 },
+      pos_distribution: { NOUN: 0.20, VERB: 0.16, ADJ: 0.14, ADV: 0.12, PUNCT: 0.17 },
+      // Very low dialogue — interior monologue and narration dominate
+      dialogue_ratio: 0.09,
+      // High first-person — unreliable narrator confessional style
+      first_person_ratio: 0.52,
+    },
+    distinctive_vocab: [
+      { term: "phantasm", score: 0.92 },
+      { term: "melancholy", score: 0.89 },
+      { term: "pallid", score: 0.87 },
+    ],
+    semantic_centroid: Array(768).fill(0),
+    embedding_umap_2d: {
+      // Lower-right: solitary, dark, intense
+      centroid: [3.1, -3.2],
+      spread: 1.2,
+    },
+  },
+};

| 2026-07-12 | Hand-rolled dark-mode toggle (no next-themes) | One class + localStorage + a 3-line pre-paint script; not worth a dependency |
| 2026-07-12 | Author list fetches `GET /api/authors` with seed fallback | Live-uploaded authors appear; selector never renders empty if the API is down |
